subject: Biology
title: Ecological Succession
description: A micro-lesson that makes Ecological Succession usable.

hook.text: After a volcanic eruption, bare rock seems lifeless. But life returns, slowly and predictably.

buildup.text: Pioneer species like lichens colonize first, breaking rock into soil over many years.

discovery.text: Grasses and shrubs follow, then small trees, then a mature forest—each stage building on the last.

twist.text: Wildfires aren't always bad. Some ecosystems need fire to clear old growth and trigger new seeds.

climax.text: Succession shows that ecosystems are never truly static. They're always in slow motion.

punchline.text: Nature always rebuilds. It just takes patience.

quiz.question: What are pioneer species?
options: The first organisms to colonize a bare area
The largest predators in an ecosystem
